name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram Millvale, Pennsylvania — MoneyGram is a money transfer service operating through retail locations like CVS, offering domestic and internationa...
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer service that operates through a network of retail partner locations, including the CVS pharmacy chain. The company specializes in enabling customers to send and receive money domestically and internationally, positioning itself as a convenient alternative to traditional banking for transfer needs. With locations embedded in everyday retail environments, MoneyGram aims to make financial transactions accessible to underbanked and convenience-seeking consumers.

At participating locations like the CVS at 3075 W Liberty Ave in Pittsburgh, MoneyGram provides money sending and receiving services along with related cash-based financial services. The specific service offerings at individual locations include money transfer capabilities, with the company emphasizing ease of use both online and through in-person transactions. Customers can manage transfers through multiple channels, though the website focuses primarily on the in-person location experience and basic service availability.

MoneyGram distinguishes itself through its retail partnership model, placing services in high-traffic locations with extended operating hours (8 AM to 10 PM weekdays at the Pittsburgh CVS location). This strategy makes money transfer services accessible during evening and weekend hours when traditional banking services are unavailable. The company also maintains a customer service phone line and requires only a legal form of ID for cash-related services, lowering barriers to access compared to some financial institutions.

The primary limitation of MoneyGram based on available information is the opacity around specific fees, exchange rates, and service costs. While the website effectively communicates location and hours, it provides minimal detail about pricing structures or comparative advantages over competitors. For consumers, this means additional research or direct contact is necessary before committing to a transfer, and fees may be higher than digital alternatives or traditional banking channels.
pros: Extended operating hours until 10 PM on weekdays, accommodating working customers outside standard banking hours
Weekend service availability (8 AM-6 PM Saturday and Sunday) for non-traditional schedules
Retail location convenience through CVS partnership, integrating services into everyday shopping destinations
Multiple transaction channels including both online and in-person options for flexibility
Simple ID requirement for cash services, removing barriers for underbanked populations
Established, recognizable brand with national network of locations
Toll-free customer support line available for account and service questions
cons: Website provides no fee transparency or pricing information, requiring customers to contact directly or visit in person
No details on exchange rates for international transfers or how they compare to competitors
Limited service information on the website; specifics about money order options, bill payment, or wire transfer fees are absent
Dependent on retail partner hours and policies, which may vary by location and restrict actual service availability
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers who need convenient cash-based money transfer without traditional banking relationships
Working professionals seeking evening or weekend money transfer options outside standard 9-5 banking hours
Customers sending domestic or international remittances who prefer in-person transactions over digital platforms
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers who prioritize convenience and extended hours over competitive pricing, particularly those without traditional bank accounts seeking to send or receive money. The primary caveat is the complete absence of fee and rate information on their website, making it essential to contact the specific location or call their service line to compare costs before using their services, as competitors may offer significantly lower fees.
services: Customer service via toll-free phone line
